vitals.terrain: Rural inland hills and coastal roads

course_description.overview: Multi-stage cyclosportive event held annually in October on the Balearic island of Ibiza. The event features four stages combining competitive timed sections with recreational cycling through the island's interior and coastal terrain.

key_segments.name: Stage 1
key_segments.description: Southern Ibiza loop with start and finish in Sant Antoni. Includes a timed section at Cala Vadella that determines classification.

key_segments.name: Stage 2
key_segments.description: Longest stage featuring two timed climbs.

key_segments.name: Prologue
key_segments.description: Competitive time trial bordering Portmany Bay.

key_segments.name: Stage 4
key_segments.description: Final stage between San Rafael and Santa Inés with a 14 km circuit (3 laps) between Santa Inés and San Mateo.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Moderate difficulty cyclosportive combining recreational touring with competitive timed sections. The event incorporates ~2,000 m of elevation gain across four stages, with terrain ranging from coastal routes to inland hill climbs. The mix of timed and untimed sections allows riders of varying abilities to participate.

logistics.getting_there: Ibiza is accessible via international airport with connections to mainland Spain and Europe. Ferry services also available from Valencia and other Mediterranean ports.
logistics.accommodation: Cycling Friendly certified accommodations available throughout Ibiza. Sant Antoni serves as a key hub for the event.
logistics.registration_details: Registration for the 2026 edition opens in June. Multiple participation options available: full event, competitive sections only, or cyclosportive sections only. Registration deadline for 2026 is March 27, 2026. Event dates in 2026 are October 10-12.
logistics.bike_logistics: Standard road bike event; no specific bike rental or logistics services mentioned in available sources.
logistics.local_considerations: October timing provides favorable Mediterranean weather. Event is established and well-organized with multiple participation categories to accommodate different skill levels and interests.

final_verdict.one_liner: Accessible Mediterranean island cyclosportive offering scenic coastal and inland riding with flexible participation options.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Recreational cyclists seeking a well-organized European cyclosportive with Mediterranean charm; riders interested in exploring Ibiza's cycling infrastructure; those wanting a less demanding alternative to major Alpine events.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those prioritizing scenic island riding and flexible event formats over prestige or extreme challenge. The October timing and multiple participation categories make it accessible to varied skill levels.
pro_tips: Register in June when registration opens to secure your spot
Choose your participation level (full event, competitive only, or cyclosportive only) based on your fitness and goals
Book Cycling Friendly certified accommodation in advance, particularly in Sant Antoni
Arrive early to acclimate to the island and scout course sections

videos.title: XVIII edición de la Vuelta cicloturista Ibiza Campagnolo 2021
videos.description:
La XVIII edición de la cicloturista Ibiza Campagnolo ya se ha celebrado y aquí podéis disfrutar del resumen de sus cuatro etapas. 

Esta, ha sido una edición dedicada a las mujeres ciclistas y a la categoría de ciclismo adaptado, homenaje al medallista olímpico Ricardo Ten, que ha alcanzado su propio récord con más de 360 participantes.

Nuria Tauler y Marc Torres se han proclamado campeones absolutos de la Vuelta Cicloturista a Ibiza Campagnolo 2021 tras la disputa de la cuarta y última etapa, una contrarreloj individual por las calles de Sant Antoni que se adjudicaron Sandra Alonso y el ciclista ibicenco.

rider_intel.search_text: The available video transcript for the Vuelta Cicloturista a Ibiza contains only fragmentary audio elements including music, applause, and disconnected words, without any substantive rider commentary or race analysis. No specific information about the 174-mile course through the Balearic Islands, terrain conditions, elevation challenges, or racing strategies could be extracted from the transcript. The video appears to be primarily visual content without detailed verbal discussion of race features, logistics, or rider experiences on Ibiza's roads and countryside.
